Ravr Menra
('rrrrrnel
Fnrn Por-rrrcAr- PnAcrtcES CorvrMts.sroN
January L7, 1996
Donald E. Bowling
Sj-erra County Board of Supervisors
District Number 4
Post Office Box 239
Downieville, California 
95936
Re: Your Request for Advice
Our File No. A-95-4O2
Dear Mr. Bowling:
This is in response to your request for advice regarding your
responsibitities as a public official 
under the conflict-of-
intLrest provisions of the Political Reform Act (the rras;rr1.1
OUESTTON
Does your familial relationship to your niece or employmelt
relationshlp with her parents prohibit you from participating in
Iand use decisions that affect the subdivision where your niece
resides?
CONCLUSION
No, your familial relationship to your niece does not
prohibii you from participating in land use decisions affecting
Lhe subdivision where your niece resides. AIso, your emplolnnent
relationship does not prohibit your participation provided the
decisions will have no material financial effect on your
employer's business.
1 corr"rnment Code Sections 81OOO-910L5. A11 statutory references
are to the Government Code unless otherwise indicated. 
Commissi-on
regulatj.ons appear at 2 California Code of Regulations, Sections
L8000-18995.
428 J SrnEer, Surre 800, Sncnervr.Nro' Certt'onNrn 95814 (916\ 322-5660


File No. A-95-402
Page 2
FACTS
The Sierra county Board of Supervisors, of which you are a
member, witl be considering the following land use ordinances
pertaining to lots within a housing subdivision located in Eastern
sierra county: rezoning of lots from rrcFrr (General Forest zone)
to R-l- and/or RR1 (Residential or Rural, respectively), adopting a
nondomestic animal maintenance overlay zone, adopting modification
to building ordinances affecting structures for maintenance of
nondonestic animals, and possible nergers of adjacent lots to
increase lot acreage' There j-s a serious conf I j'ct within the
subdivision between those $/ho maintain horses on their property
and those who do not.
Your brother's daughter maintains a horse on her property
which is within the subdivision. You have no economic
relationship lrith this niece or any of her irnrnediate family and no
ownership or econonic interest in any parcel of real property
within tire subdivis j.on issue.2 You do work part time for a
business establishment ohrned by your brother and his wife, but no
zoning decision by the board of supervisors with respect to the
subdivision wouId have any economic effect on either your brother
or your brother's business for which you are ernployed.
ANALYS I S
Section 87100 of the Act prohibits any public official 
frorn
making, participating in makinq, or otherwise using his or her
official position to inftuence a governmental decision in which
the official 
has a financial interest.
Section 87103 specifies that a public official 
has a
financial interest in a decision if it is reasonably foreseeable
that the decision will have a material financial effect,
distinguishable from the effect on the public generally, on the
official- or a nember of his or her inmediate fanily or on:
(a) Any business entity in r.rhich the public
official 
has a direct or indirect investnent lrorth one
thousand dotlars ($1,000) or more.
(b) Any real property in which the public official
has a direct or indirect interest worth one thousand
dollars (S1,0oo) or more.
(c) Any source of income, other than gifts and
other than loans by a conmercial lending institution in
2 Yo, mentioned in your request for advice that your niece and
rrher farnityrt own proPerty in the subdivision. For the purposes of
this letter, I am assuming you mean your niece's spouse and
dependent cilildren own the property, not her father and his wife,
who own the business for which you work part tirne.


File No. A-9 5-4 02
Page 3
the regular course of business on terms avail-able to the
public without regard to official 
status, . aggregat ing
Lwo hundred fifty dollars ($250) or more j-n value
provided to, received by or proDised to the public
tfticiat 
lrithin 12 months prior to the time when the
decision is rnade.
(d) Any business entity in which the public
official 
is a director, officer, partner, trustee,
employee, or holds any positj'on of management '
(e) Any donor of, or any intermediary or agent for
a donor of, a gift or gifts aggregating two hundred
f j.fty dollars ($250) or more in value prov!ded to,
received by, or prornised to the public officiaL within
12 months prior to the tihe when the decision is made'
(Section 87103. )
You nay be disquatified if a decision viI1 result in your
personal "x!"t="=, income, assets, or liabilities, 
or those of
|our inmeaiate famity, increasing or decreasing by at least.$250'
lnegul,ation :^8702.1(;) (4).) 
"Immediate fanify'! is defined in
s""€io., 82o2g as the official's 
spouse and dependent children'
Because a public official's 
niece j-s not a nember of the
oiti"i"lrr^ 
imrnediate farnily for purposes of the Act, the fanilial
ieiationship between you and your niece does not, by itself,
create a conflict of interest for you.
You would be required to disqualify yourself from decisions
affecting your niece only if your niece has been a source of
income to you in the sun of $250 or more within the 12 months
preceding Lhe decision. Any disqual-ifying income from your niece
i,routa neia to be from a source other than gifts because gifts frorn
your niece totafling $250 or more do not create a disqualifying
interest. 
(Section 82028(b) (3).) 
Consequently, absent some other
disqualifying financial interest as set forth in Section 87LO3,
you may participate in decisions concerning your niece's
subdivision.
The facts you have provided indicate that you do not have
real property, a business entity or source of income which will be
affected by the decision. (Section 87103(a), (b) and (c).) 
You
mentioned that you work for a business establishment ouned by your
niece's father. 
The business is a disqualifying interest if you
are an employee of the business or if your part tine income fron
the business totalled $250 or nore in the past 12 nonths.
(Section 871-03 (c) and (d).) 
As such, you may not participate in
any decision which will have a foreseeable material financial
effect on the business. You have indicated in your request that
the business hri1l not be affected at all by the zoning decisions
regardinq the subdivision. Assuming this is true, then the
decisions will not have a foreseeable, material financial effect
on your economic j.nterest in the business and you may participate.


Fil-e No. A-95-4o2
Page 4
If you have any further questions, pfease feel free to
contact me at (916) 322-5660-
Sincerely,
Steven G. Church\.ie1]
SGC : LR: AK
Genera I
',/L
counsel , LegaI Division

CONCLUSION: No, your familial relationship to your niece does not

Lhe subdivision where your niece resides. AIso, your emplolnnent
relationship does not prohibit your participation provided the
decisions will have no material financial effect on your
employer's business.
